question_title: Will average hourly earnings for all employees on U.S. private nonfarm payrolls rise by more than 0.3% from February 2026 to March 2026?
summary: The question asks whether March 2026 AHE MoM growth will strictly exceed 0.3%. From 49 months of BLS data (Jan 2022–Feb 2026), 34 months (69.4%) exceeded this threshold. The Feb-to-Mar seasonal pattern is especially strong: all 4 observed years exceeded 0.3% (min 0.36%). In dollar terms, the threshold requires only a $0.12 increase from $37.32, and all 4 historical Feb-to-Mar increases were $0.12–$0.17. Wage growth remains sticky at +0.4% MoM in Feb 2026 despite labor market cooling (payrolls -92K, unemployment 4.4%). The conditional probability after a >0.3% month is actually lower (60.6%), providing modest offset. I anchor near the ~69% base rate with slight seasonal uplift to 70%.

factors.description: Overall historical base rate: 69.4% of months (34/49) had MoM AHE growth > 0.3% from Jan 2022-Feb 2026
Feb-to-Mar seasonal pattern: All 4 observed Feb-to-Mar transitions (2022-2025) exceeded 0.3%, with minimum 0.36%
Sticky wage growth: Feb 2026 AHE +0.4% MoM and 3.8% YoY. Low quit rate and frozen job market suggest wage stickiness persists
Labor market cooling: Nonfarm payrolls fell 92K in Feb 2026, unemployment up to 4.4%, hiring demand softening
Conditional on previous month >0.3%: When prior month exceeded 0.3%, only 60.6% of following months also exceeded - slight mean-reversion signal
Dollar-value threshold is modest: Need only $0.12 increase from $37.32. All 4 historical Feb-to-Mar increases were $0.12-$0.17

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.70 (70% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:
- **Base rate anchor**: 69.4% of the 49 months from 2022–2026 had AHE MoM growth above 0.3%
- **Seasonal strength**: All 4 Feb-to-Mar transitions in the dataset exceeded 0.3% (minimum 0.36%), though the small sample limits confidence
- **Current environment**: Wages remain sticky at +0.4% MoM despite significant labor market weakening (payrolls -92K in Feb). Wage changes typically lag employment by months
- **Achievable threshold**: March AHE only needs to reach $37.44 (a $0.12 increase), well within historical Feb-to-Mar norms of $0.12–$0.17
- **Modest offset**: The conditional probability after a >0.3% month is 60.6%, and labor market deterioration provides some downward pressure
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will average hourly earnings for all employees on U.S. private nonfarm payrolls rise by more than 0.3% from February 2026 to March 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42797)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the first BLS Employment Situation release for March 2026, published before April 4, 2026, reports a month-over-month increase in average hourly earnings for all employees on private nonfarm payrolls that is strictly above 0.3000%. It resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is the BLS Employment Situation release page at https://www.bls.gov/news.release/empsit.htm
fine_print:
Specifically, this will be calculated by dividing the average hourly earnings figure for March 2026 by the average hourly earnings figure for February 2026. Please note that this may be different from the rounded figure presented by BLS and it may be a preliminary figure.

If the main release page is unavailable, use the BLS PDF or table B-3. Candidate Sources: BLS PDF release at https://www.bls.gov/news.release/pdf/empsit.pdf | BLS table B-3 at https://www.bls.gov/news.release/empsit.t03.htm
